growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: fierer.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: Modified basic medium (BM), citrate as C source (oxic AMAO dynamics)

up.units: mM (total consumed over incubation; time not tabulated in text)

sec.units: mM (max transient; = 13.1% of 20 mM added ammonium)
produced (end product; not quantified in main text)

growth_records.cite: Lei et al. Acetone-mediated ammonium oxidation to dinitrogen by Zobellella taiwanensis bacteria. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf230. [PMC12596615]
growth_records.snippet: Subsequent time-course experiments (33.3 mM citrate, 20 mM NH4+) under oxic conditions showed a transient accumulation of acetoxime (up to 13.1% of the added ammonium)... final growth yield (OD600) of 1.43 +/- 0.15. ...acetone... accounted
growth_records.notes: Base BM per Methods: 0.66 g/L (NH4)2SO4, 5.72 g/L sodium citrate dihydrate, 0.4 g/L K2HPO4, 0.02 g/L MgSO4.7H2O, 0.1 ml/L trace elements, pH 8.5-9.0 (NaOH); for this oxic AMAO dynamics experiment carbon/N were modified to 33.3 mM citrate and 10 mM (NH4)2SO4 (=20 mM NH4+-N). pH midpoint 8.75 taken from stated 8.5-9.0. Acetone secretion = 0.163 x 33.3 mM = 5.43 mM. Acetoxime = 0.131 x 20 mM = 2.62 mM. No growth rate or doubling time reported (growth quantified only as endpoint OD600). Substrate consumption times not tabulated in text (figure bar graphs), so per-gDW/h fluxes not derivable.

growth_records.medium: Modified BM, sucrose C source + nitrite electron acceptor (anoxic)

sec.units: mM (max; = up to 20.4% of 8.3 mM consumed sucrose)
produced (highest N2 production rate among acceptors; not numerically tabulated)

growth_records.cite: Lei et al. Acetone-mediated ammonium oxidation to dinitrogen by Zobellella taiwanensis bacteria. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf230. [PMC12596615]
growth_records.snippet: when fed with sucrose, ammonium, and any of these electron acceptors, both transient acetoxime production and acetone production, up to 16.9%-20.4% of the consumed sucrose, were observed... 10 mM nitrite (B)... incubated at 30 C for 72 h (N
growth_records.notes: Anoxic AMAO with sucrose. Acetone = 0.204 x 8.3 mM = 1.69 mM (upper bound of 16.9-20.4% range shared across B-D). NH4+-N 10 mM = 5 mM (NH4)2SO4. Incubation 72 h for nitrite. No growth rate/OD reported for this specific panel; nitrite gave the highest N2 production rate (near-stoichiometric NH4+/NO2- exhaustion at equal 10 mM). Per-gDW fluxes not derivable (biomass not given).

growth_records.medium: Modified BM, sucrose C source + nitrate electron acceptor (anoxic)

sec.units: mM (max; up to 20.4% of 8.3 mM consumed sucrose)

growth_records.cite: Lei et al. Acetone-mediated ammonium oxidation to dinitrogen by Zobellella taiwanensis bacteria. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf230. [PMC12596615]
growth_records.snippet: 10 mM nitrate (C)... under anoxic conditions... incubated at 30 C for... 96 h (NaNO3)... acetone production, up to 16.9%-20.4% of the consumed sucrose.
growth_records.notes: Same as nitrite condition but nitrate as electron acceptor, 96 h. Acetone up to 0.204 x 8.3 = 1.69 mM. No OD/growth rate for this panel; per-gDW fluxes not derivable.

growth_records.medium: Modified BM, sucrose C source + ferric citrate electron acceptor (anoxic)

sec.units: mM (max; up to 20.4% of 8.3 mM consumed sucrose)

growth_records.cite: Lei et al. Acetone-mediated ammonium oxidation to dinitrogen by Zobellella taiwanensis bacteria. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf230. [PMC12596615]
growth_records.snippet: 50 mM ferric citrate (D) under anoxic conditions... incubated at 30 C for... 144 h (ferric citrate)... Z. taiwanensis was also able to reduce ferric sulfate, ferric citrate, and ferric hydroxide.
growth_records.notes: Anoxic AMAO with Fe(III) as electron acceptor, 144 h. Acetone up to 0.204 x 8.3 = 1.69 mM. Also reduces Fe2(SO4)3, Fe(OH)3, Fe2O3 (Fig 1E). No OD/growth rate; per-gDW fluxes not derivable.

growth_records.medium: Modified BM, pyruvate C source, 0.5 mM NH4+-N (oxic)

sec.units: 5.2-17.0% of consumed pyruvate (proportion declines with higher NH4+)

growth_records.cite: Lei et al. Acetone-mediated ammonium oxidation to dinitrogen by Zobellella taiwanensis bacteria. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf230. [PMC12596615]
growth_records.snippet: we cultured Z. taiwanensis with 5 mM pyruvate-C and varying ammonium-N levels (0.5, 5, and 50 mM)... growth yields (OD600) of 0.38 +/- 0.02, 0.25 +/- 0.01, and 0.26 +/- 0.01 for oxic cultures... acetone production accounting for 5.2%-17.0%
growth_records.notes: Oxic, 0.5 mM NH4+-N. 5 mM pyruvate-C / 3 C per pyruvate = 1.67 mM pyruvate. Final OD600 0.38 +/- 0.02. Acetone 5.2-17.0% of consumed pyruvate (range shared across the 0.5/5/50 mM NH4+ series; proportion declines as NH4+ increases). No growth rate/doubling time; per-gDW fluxes not derivable.

growth_records.medium: Modified BM, pyruvate C source, 5 mM NH4+-N (oxic)
growth_records.cite: Lei et al. Acetone-mediated ammonium oxidation to dinitrogen by Zobellella taiwanensis bacteria. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf230. [PMC12596615]
growth_records.snippet: growth yields (OD600) of 0.38 +/- 0.02, 0.25 +/- 0.01, and 0.26 +/- 0.01 for oxic cultures (0.5, 5, and 50 mM NH4+-N, respectively).
growth_records.notes: Oxic, 5 mM NH4+-N. Final OD600 0.25 +/- 0.01. Acetone 5.2-17.0% of consumed pyruvate (series range). No growth rate; per-gDW fluxes not derivable.

growth_records.medium: Modified BM, pyruvate C source, 50 mM NH4+-N (oxic)

up.units: partial (residual NH4+ observed at 50 mM due to acidification/pH<7 inhibition)

sec.units: 5.2-17.0% of consumed pyruvate (lower proportion at high NH4+/low C:N)

growth_records.cite: Lei et al. Acetone-mediated ammonium oxidation to dinitrogen by Zobellella taiwanensis bacteria. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf230. [PMC12596615]
growth_records.snippet: in some tests, particularly those with 50 mM ammonium, residual ammonium was consistently observed. This persistence likely resulted from progressive acidification during ammonium oxidation... pH values below 7.0 were found to significantly
growth_records.notes: Oxic, 50 mM NH4+-N. Final OD600 0.26 +/- 0.01. Ammonium only partially consumed (residual) due to H+ release lowering pH below 7 (inhibitory). No growth rate; per-gDW fluxes not derivable.

growth_records.medium: Modified BM, pyruvate C source, 0.5 mM NH4+-N, nitrate acceptor (anoxic)
growth_records.cite: Lei et al. Acetone-mediated ammonium oxidation to dinitrogen by Zobellella taiwanensis bacteria. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf230. [PMC12596615]
growth_records.snippet: 0.03 +/- 0.00, 0.12 +/- 0.02, and 0.32 +/- 0.05 for anoxic cultures... maintaining a nitrate-N/ammonium-N ratio of 0.6... These results suggested that ammonium oxidation may promote Z. taiwanensis growth under anoxic conditions.
growth_records.notes: Anoxic, 0.5 mM NH4+-N, nitrate-N = 0.6 x 0.5 = 0.3 mM. Final OD600 0.03 +/- 0.00. Anoxic yields rose with ammonium (0.03->0.12->0.32 for 0.5/5/50 mM NH4+), indicating NH4+ oxidation supports anoxic growth. No growth rate; per-gDW fluxes not derivable.

growth_records.medium: Modified BM, pyruvate C source, 5 mM NH4+-N, nitrate acceptor (anoxic)
growth_records.cite: Lei et al. Acetone-mediated ammonium oxidation to dinitrogen by Zobellella taiwanensis bacteria. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf230. [PMC12596615]
growth_records.snippet: 0.03 +/- 0.00, 0.12 +/- 0.02, and 0.32 +/- 0.05 for anoxic cultures (0.5, 5, and 50 mM NH4+-N, respectively).
growth_records.notes: Anoxic, 5 mM NH4+-N, nitrate-N = 3 mM. Final OD600 0.12 +/- 0.02. No growth rate; per-gDW fluxes not derivable.

growth_records.medium: Modified BM, pyruvate C source, 50 mM NH4+-N, nitrate acceptor (anoxic)

sec.units: 5.2-17.0% of consumed pyruvate (lower at high NH4+)

growth_records.cite: Lei et al. Acetone-mediated ammonium oxidation to dinitrogen by Zobellella taiwanensis bacteria. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf230. [PMC12596615]
growth_records.snippet: 0.03 +/- 0.00, 0.12 +/- 0.02, and 0.32 +/- 0.05 for anoxic cultures... ammonium oxidation may promote Z. taiwanensis growth under anoxic conditions.
growth_records.notes: Anoxic, 50 mM NH4+-N, nitrate-N = 30 mM. Highest anoxic yield OD600 0.32 +/- 0.05. No growth rate; per-gDW fluxes not derivable.

growth_records.medium: Modified BM, pyruvate C source, nitrate only (no ammonium) - denitrification control (anoxic)
growth_records.cite: Lei et al. Acetone-mediated ammonium oxidation to dinitrogen by Zobellella taiwanensis bacteria. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf230. [PMC12596615]
growth_records.snippet: In control experiments with nitrate-N (0.3, 3, or 30 mM) in the absence of ammonium, growth yields were lower (0.01 +/- 0.00, 0.03 +/- 0.02, and 0.09 +/- 0.03, respectively), confirming negligible denitrification-driven growth in the absenc
growth_records.notes: Anoxic denitrification-only controls (no ammonium): OD600 yields 0.01/0.03/0.09 for 0.3/3/30 mM nitrate-N. Low yields confirm ammonium oxidation (AMAO) is the main growth-supporting process anoxically. Three nitrate levels combined into one control record. No growth rate; per-gDW fluxes not derivable.
